ops.text:
RESULTS + VERDICT (close-out).

RUN: qwen3-8b LoRA SFT, WatcherRecipe.default (rank32/lr1e-4/batch16/2ep), 196 steps. Split: train 1559 / val 137 / test 202 (E24 single-select eval, apples-to-apples). Clean exit, no SpendGuard fire.

FT METRICS (E24-exact: bootstrap CI 1000×seed24, 15-bin repo ECE, Brier):
- accuracy = 0.6089 (best of ANY candidate: E24 baselines were 0.559–0.579, opus 0.559, floor 0.574)
- accuracy 95% CI = [0.5396, 0.6782]
- ECE = 0.0450 (train.py 10-bin variant: 0.0521) — well under the 0.10 bar
- Brier multiclass 0.5304 / top-label 0.2180 (best proper scores seen; beats opus 0.585 / 0.226)
FLOOR (always-option-0): acc 0.5743 (116/202), ECE 0.4257, Brier 0.851/0.426.

GATE (pre-registered): NOT MET.
- Beats floor point estimate: 0.609 > 0.574 (+0.0347) ✓
- 95% CI excludes floor: NO — CI lower 0.5396 < 0.5743 (floor sits inside the CI)
- ECE ≤ 0.10: 0.045 ✓
- Paired FT-vs-floor Δacc = +0.0347, 95% CI [0.000, 0.0742] — DOES NOT exclude zero (touches 0)
=> gate_met = FALSE. The +3.5pt accuracy lift over the trivial position prior is WITHIN THE NOISE FLOOR at n=202. Reported as "within noise floor," not a win/promotion.

INTERPRETATION: Cleanest negative-with-signal in the campaign. The FT genuinely improves calibration and proper scores (ECE 0.045, Brier 0.530/0.218 — best on record) and posts the highest accuracy point estimate, but top-1 accuracy is not statistically separable from "always option 0" at this eval size. The ~19% custom-answer deferral residual on eval (24% overall) is the modeling ceiling E24 flagged. Resolving the +3.5pt trend needs a larger/cleaner eval (post-T3 frame) — a promotion verdict waits for that per campaign rule.

CROSS-CORPUS (item #2) — NOT RUN, shape change. VERIFIED via readlink: all 18 /Users/yasyf/.cc-pool/accounts/acct-*/projects symlink to the identical /Users/yasyf/.claude/projects (local, not a distinct corpus). Standing user rule forbids analyzing local ~/.claude; E24 hit and documented this same wall (mirror-only). pool_eval = {n:0}. Options surfaced to main: (a) accept mirror-only per E24 precedent; (b) explicitly authorize a local-~/.claude eval-only transfer set (deduped by (session_id,tool_use_id,question_index) vs the 2018 mirror rows) as a rule override; (c) point E29 at a genuinely distinct corpus (another user's mirror); (d) drop the supplemental.

SPEND: Tinker fit cumulative $0.486 at step 171/196 → ~$0.56 at completion; + checkpoint/test scoring (forward-only, small); total ~$0.6–0.9, well under the $9 cap (never fired). Exact final actual not persisted — train.py rmtree's the RunSink journal on exit; consider persisting outcome.report cost next revision.

ARTIFACTS (raw, for the verify lane to re-derive):
- E29/out/train/preds_test.jsonl (202 per-decision option prob vectors — the key artifact)
- E29/out/e29_gate.json (E24-exact accuracy/CI/ECE/Brier + paired delta + gate)
- E29/out/train/e29_report.json (train.py summary)
- E29/out/{prep_report.json,train.jsonl,test.jsonl}
- E29/bin/{e29lib.py, train.py} (adapted), E29/bin/e29_score.py (new offline scorer)
